text: Lesson 39
zh: 第39课

text: What every writer wants
zh: 每个作家想要的是什么

text: How do professional writers ignore what they were taught at school about writing?
zh: 专业作家是怎样抛开他们在学校所学的写作规则的？

text: I have known very few writers,
zh: 我认识的作家不多，

text: but those I have known and whom I respect, confess at once that they have little idea where they are going when they first set pen to paper.
zh: 但我认识并尊敬的那几位，都立刻承认，他们提笔之初，几乎不知道自己要写向何方。

text: They have a character, perhaps two;
zh: 他们脑中有一个角色，也许两个；

text: they are in that condition of eager discomfort which passes for inspiration all admit radical changes of destination once the journey has begun;
zh: 他们处于一种急切而不安的状态，这种状态常被人们称作灵感——所有人都承认，一旦旅程开始，最终的方向往往会彻底改变；

text: one, to my certain knowledge, spent nine months on a novel about Kashmir,
zh: 据我确切了解，有一位作家花了九个月写一部关于克什米尔的小说，

text: then reset the whole thing in the Scottish Highlands.
zh: 后来又把整个故事的背景改到了苏格兰高地。

text: I never heard of anyone making a 'skeleton', as we were taught at school.
zh: 我从未听说过有谁像我们在学校被教导的那样，先列一个'提纲'。

text: In the breaking and remaking, in the timing interweaving, beginning afresh,
zh: 在不断打破重塑、时序交织、重新开始的过程中，

text: the writer comes to discern things in his material which were not consciously in his mind when he began.
zh: 作家逐渐察觉到材料中那些他动笔之初并未有意识想到的东西。

text: This organic process, often leading to moments of extraordinary self-discovery, is of an indescribable fascination.
zh: 这种有机的创作过程常常带来非凡的自我发现时刻，其魅力难以言喻。

text: A blurred image appears; he adds a brushstroke and another and it is gone
zh: 一个模糊的形象浮现出来；他添上一笔，又添一笔，形象却消失了，

text: but something was there, and he will not rest till he has captured it.
zh: 但确实曾有什么东西存在过，他不会罢休，直到将其捕捉到手。

text: Sometimes the yeast within a writer outlives a book he has written.
zh: 有时，作家心中的那股酵素会比他已写成的书存活得更久。

text: I have heard of writers who read nothing but their own books;
zh: 我听说过有些作家只读自己的书；

text: like adolescents they stand before the mirror,
zh: 他们像青春期的少年一样站在镜子前，

text: and still cannot fathom the exact outline of the vision before them.
zh: 却依然无法看清眼前那幻象的确切轮廓。

text: For the same reason, writers talk interminably about their own books,
zh: 出于同样的原因，作家们没完没了地谈论自己的书，

text: winkling out hidden meanings, super-imposing new ones,
zh: 从中挖掘出隐藏的含义，再叠加上新的含义，

text: begging response from those around them.
zh: 恳求周围人的回应。

text: Of course a writer doing this is misunderstood: he might as well try to explain a crime or a love affair.
zh: 当然，这样做的作家常常不被理解：他不如去解释一桩罪案或一段恋情。

text: He is also, incidentally, an unforgivable bore.
zh: 顺带一提，他也令人无法忍受地乏味。

text: This temptation to cover the distance between himself and the reader,
zh: 这种想要拉近自己与读者之间距离的诱惑，

text: to study his image in the sight of those who do not know him, can be his undoing: he has begun to write to please.
zh: 想借那些不了解他的人的目光来审视自己形象的诱惑，可能会毁了他：因为他已经开始为取悦而写作。

text: A young English writer made the pertinent observation a year or two back
zh: 一两年前，一位年轻的英国作家提出了一个中肯的看法，

text: that the talent goes into the first draft, and the art into the drafts that follow.
zh: 即天赋体现在初稿之中，而艺术则体现在之后的修改稿里。

text: For this reason also the writer, like any other artist,
zh: 正因如此，作家和其他任何艺术家一样，

text: has no resting place, no crowd or movement in which he may take comfort,
zh: 没有安身之所，没有可以从中获得慰藉的群体或潮流，

text: no judgment from outside which can replace the judgment from within.
zh: 也没有任何外部评判能够取代内心的评判。

text: A writer makes order out of the anarchy of his heart;
zh: 作家从内心的混乱中创造出秩序；

text: he submits himself to a more ruthless discipline than any critic dreamed of,
zh: 他让自己服从于一种比任何评论家所能想象的都更严苛的纪律，

text: and when he flirts with fame, he is taking time off from living with himself,
zh: 当他与名声调情时，他就是在从与自己相处的时间里偷懒，

text: from the search for what his world contains at its inmost point.
zh: 从探寻他所处世界最深处所蕴含之物的过程中偷懒。
